@@ -28,7 +28,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","title":"Create onboard TUI shell script","description":"# Task: Create Onboard TUI Shell Script ## Description Build the interactive onboarding TUI that teaches users the ACFS workflow. ## Location Install to: `~/.local/bin/onboard` ## UX Flow ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Welcome to ACFS Onboarding │ │ │ │ Choose a lesson: │ │ │ │ [1] Welcome & Overview ✓ completed │ │ [2] Linux Navigation (5 min) ● in progress│ │ [3] SSH & Persistence │ │ [4] tmux Basics │ │ [5] Agent Commands (cc, cod, gmi) │ │ [6] NTM Command Center │ │ [7] NTM Prompt Palette │ │ [8] The Flywheel Loop │ │ │ │ [q] Quit [r] Restart from beginning │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ## Implementation Options ### Option A: Pure Bash + gum Use charmbracelet/gum for TUI elements: ```bash gum choose "Welcome" "Linux Navigation" "SSH & Persistence" ... ``` ### Option B: Simple menu Plain bash with read: ```bash while true; do clear echo "ACFS Onboarding" echo "1) Welcome" echo "2) Linux Navigation" ... read -p "Choose: " choice done ``` ## Lesson Format Each lesson: 1. Brief explanation 2. Command to try 3. Expected output 4. Checkpoint/quiz ## Progress Tracking Store in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json: ```json { "completed": [1, 2, 3], "current": 4, "started_at": "2025-12-19T..." } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 8 lessons implemented - [ ] Progress saved between runs - [ ] Can be re-run anytime - [ ] Works over SSH - [ ] Clear and beginner-friendly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:17:32.895359-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:17:32.895359-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented interactive onboard TUI with: gum-based menu when available, fallback simple menu, progress tracking in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json, lesson completion markers, --reset flag, updated 1-8 numbering. Commit d96659e.","labels":["onboard","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.969656-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2cvc","title":"[EPIC] Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide","description":"# Epic: Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide ## Problem Statement Users complete the wizard and land on the celebration page, but then have NO IDEA what to do next. The wizard ends, but their journey is just beginning. Critical gaps: 1. **No daily workflow explained**: How do I start working each day? SSH in, then what? 2. **tmux/ntm confusion**: What is tmux? Why do I need it? What is ntm? The concepts are introduced without adequate context. 3. **Project creation mystery**: How do I start my OWN project? The wizard uses "my-first-project" but doesn't explain the general pattern. 4. **File system disorientation**: Where are my files? How do I find things? How do I navigate? 5. **Manual editing unknown**: What if Claude gets something wrong? How do I fix files myself? ## Background & Reasoning This is arguably the most critical gap in the entire user experience. The wizard successfully gets users SET UP, but doesn't teach them how to USE the system. They have a powerful engine room but no operator's manual. The "Your First 5 Minutes" section on the final page helps, but it's not enough. Users need: - A mental model for daily workflow - Understanding of persistence (why tmux/ntm matter) - Confidence to start their own projects - Basic navigation skills ## User Story As a user who just completed setup, I want to understand how to use my new environment daily, So that I can start building projects with confidence. ## Success Criteria 1. User knows their daily workflow: SSH → ntm attach → work 2. User understands why tmux sessions matter (persistence) 3. User can create a new project from scratch 4. User can navigate the filesystem (cd, lsd, etc.) 5. User knows how to manually edit files if needed 6. User knows how to get back in if they close their laptop ## Scope - Create "Your Daily Workflow" guide section - Expand "Getting Back In" section - Add "Starting a New Project" guide - Add "Finding Your Way Around" filesystem basics - Add "Manual Editing" section (nano, or VS Code Remote) - Enhance Learning Hub with these practical guides ## Dependencies - Epic E: Authentication (user must be authenticated) - Epic G: Post-Install Transition (user must be reconnected as ubuntu) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Consider a dedicated "Getting Started" page vs cramming into celebration page - Workflow should be presented as numbered steps - Include visual diagrams of the workflow loop - Mobile: ensure code examples are copyable ## Technical Approach 1. Expand launch-onboarding page with comprehensive "Next Steps" 2. Create new /learn/getting-started page with full guide 3. Add filesystem basics to Linux Basics lesson 4. Add workflow diagram (SSH → ntm → cc → work → detach → exit) 5. Add manual editing section with nano instructions 6. Consider VS Code Remote SSH setup guide as optional advanced content ## Estimated Effort Large (multiple pages, significant new content) ## Priority Justification P0 (Critical) - Without this, users complete setup but cannot actually USE the system. The wizard succeeds but the product fails.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:36:41.425156-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:36:41.425156-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2cvc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:38:10.153356-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2cvc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:38:15.40484-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","title":"Set up bun workspaces for monorepo","description":"# Task: Set up Bun Workspaces ## Description Configure bun workspaces in the root package.json to manage: - apps/web (Next.js wizard website) - packages/manifest (manifest utilities) - packages/onboard (onboard TUI) ## Technical Details Root package.json should include: ```json { "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"] } ``` Each workspace needs its own package.json with appropriate name/scripts. ## Reasoning Bun workspaces allow: - Shared dependencies across packages - Single lockfile (bun.lock) - Easy cross-package imports - Consistent tooling ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Root package.json with workspaces config - [ ] Each workspace has valid package.json - [ ] `bun install` works from root - [ ] Cross-package imports resolve correctly ## Dependencies Depends on directory structure being created first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.61383-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","close_reason":"Root bun workspace set up with apps/* and packages/* workspaces","labels":["infrastructure","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.616805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2gs8","title":"Add subscription requirements badges for paid services","description":"# Task: Add Subscription Requirements Badges ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description Claude Pro and ChatGPT Pro requirements are mentioned but not prominent enough. Users sign up for OpenAI/Anthropic free tiers and then can't use the agents effectively. Need visual badges. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/accounts/page.tsx Add subscription badge to ServiceCard component for services that require paid plans: \`\`\`tsx // In ServiceCard component {service.requiresSubscription && (
Paid plan required
)} \`\`\` Update services.ts to add: \`\`\`ts interface Service { // ... existing fields requiresSubscription?: boolean; subscriptionNote?: string; // e.g., "Requires Claude Pro ($20/mo)" } \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Claude Code shows "Requires Claude Pro ($20/mo)" badge - [ ] Codex CLI shows "Requires ChatGPT Pro ($20/mo)" badge - [ ] Badge is visually distinct (amber/warning color) - [ ] Tooltip or note explains what the subscription provides - [ ] Works on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use amber color (warning but not error) - Keep it informative, not discouraging - Include monthly cost for transparency","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:48:02.03247-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:48:02.03247-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2gs8","title":"Add subscription requirements badges for paid services","description":"# Task: Add Subscription Requirements Badges ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description Claude Pro and ChatGPT Pro requirements are mentioned but not prominent enough. Users sign up for OpenAI/Anthropic free tiers and then can't use the agents effectively. Need visual badges. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/accounts/page.tsx Add subscription badge to ServiceCard component for services that require paid plans: \`\`\`tsx // In ServiceCard component {service.requiresSubscription && (
Paid plan required
)} \`\`\` Update services.ts to add: \`\`\`ts interface Service { // ... existing fields requiresSubscription?: boolean; subscriptionNote?: string; // e.g., "Requires Claude Pro ($20/mo)" } \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Claude Code shows "Requires Claude Pro ($20/mo)" badge - [ ] Codex CLI shows "Requires ChatGPT Pro ($20/mo)" badge - [ ] Badge is visually distinct (amber/warning color) - [ ] Tooltip or note explains what the subscription provides - [ ] Works on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use amber color (warning but not error) - Keep it informative, not discouraging - Include monthly cost for transparency","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:48:02.03247-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:02.613366-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2m0","title":"Onboarding polish: lesson 8 visibility + tailscale jq-optional + update warn","description":"Land small UX/robustness tweaks: ensure onboarding menus/progress account for lesson 8 (Keeping Updated); make tailscale status parsing work without jq; add warn-level output helper to update logger; and keep stack installers from forcing --yes in interactive mode.","acceptance_criteria":"- Onboard menus/progress include lesson 8 (total 9 lessons)\n- tailscale.sh parses BackendState without jq installed\n- update.sh supports warn log type\n- stack.sh only adds --yes flags when non-interactive","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:07:43.806195-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:08:02.645043-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:08:02.645043-05:00","close_reason":"Updated onboarding to include lesson 8; added jq-optional tailscale BackendState parsing; added warn log helper; and made stack installers respect interactive mode."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","title":"[ubu.2] Extend state.sh for upgrade tracking","description":"# Extend state.sh for Upgrade Tracking ## Purpose Add upgrade-specific state fields to track multi-reboot upgrade progress. ## State Schema Extensions Add new fields to state.json schema: ```json { "schema_version": 3, // Bump version for new fields // ... existing fields ... // NEW: Ubuntu upgrade tracking "ubuntu_upgrade": { "enabled": true, "started_at": "2025-12-21T10:00:00Z", "original_version": "24.04", "target_version": "25.10", "current_stage": "upgrading", "upgrade_path": ["24.04", "24.10", "25.04", "25.10"], "completed_upgrades": [ {"from": "24.04", "to": "24.10", "completed_at": "..."} ], "current_upgrade": {"from": "24.10", "to": "25.04", "started_at": "..."}, "needs_reboot": false, "resume_after_reboot": true, "last_error": null } } ``` ## Functions to Add ```bash # Initialize upgrade state state_upgrade_init() { local original_version="$1" local target_version="$2" local upgrade_path="$3" # JSON array } # Mark current upgrade as started state_upgrade_start() { local from_version="$1" local to_version="$2" } # Mark current upgrade as completed state_upgrade_complete() { local to_version="$1" } # Mark that system needs reboot state_upgrade_needs_reboot() { # Sets needs_reboot=true, resume_after_reboot=true } # Clear reboot flags after successful resume state_upgrade_resumed() { # Sets needs_reboot=false } # Check if upgrade is complete state_upgrade_is_complete() { # Returns 0 if current version >= target version } # Get current upgrade stage state_upgrade_get_stage() { # Returns: not_started | upgrading | awaiting_reboot | completed } # Record upgrade error state_upgrade_set_error() { local error_msg="$1" } # Clean up upgrade state after completion state_upgrade_cleanup() { # Remove ubuntu_upgrade section entirely } ``` ## Schema Migration Add migration from v2 to v3: - v3 is backwards compatible (new fields are optional) - Existing v2 state files work without modification - ubuntu_upgrade section only added when upgrade starts ## Why Separate State Section? The upgrade state is conceptually different from installation state: - Upgrade happens BEFORE installation - Upgrade may span multiple reboots - Upgrade state should be cleaned up after completion Keeping it in a separate section makes this clear and allows independent cleanup. ## Atomic Write Considerations All state writes already use atomic temp-file+rename pattern. This is critical for upgrade state since we're about to reboot and can't risk corrupted state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:20:15.998308-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:35:14.533749-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:35:14.533749-05:00","close_reason":"Extended state.sh with 14 upgrade tracking functions (state_upgrade_init, state_upgrade_start, state_upgrade_complete, etc.). Updated schema to v3 with backward compatibility for v2.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:23:35.258019-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yzm","title":"Enhance SSH disconnection recovery with prominent AlertCard","description":"# Task: Enhance SSH Disconnection Recovery Instructions ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu) ## Current State run-installer/page.tsx already has: - GuideCaution: "Do not close the terminal window while installation is running" - GuideTip: "If your internet connection drops, just SSH back in and run again" However, this is buried in the SimplerGuide. A more prominent AlertCard near the install command would be more reassuring. ## Enhancement Add a prominent AlertCard near the install command: ```tsx

Do not panic! If your SSH connection drops during installation:

  1. The installer keeps running on the VPS
  2. Just SSH back in using the same command
  3. Run the installer command again — it will resume where it left off

The installer is designed to be run multiple times safely. If anything fails, you can always re-run it.

``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Prominent AlertCard near the install command (not buried in SimplerGuide) - [ ] Explicitly says "do not panic" - [ ] Explains installer keeps running - [ ] Explains safe to re-run (idempotent) - [ ] Reassuring tone with info variant (blue/calming) ## Note This elevates existing content from SimplerGuide to a more visible location.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:20.981302-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:24:47.839312-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:24:47.839312-05:00","close_reason":"Added prominent AlertCard with Wifi icon near install command explaining connection drop recovery: (1) installer keeps running, (2) SSH back in, (3) re-run safely"}
